title: ML coding consolidation + design pattern review
focus: Re-implement one classifier with a clean OO API. Reflect on SOLID violations.

items.label: Code k-NN classifier from scratch
interviewQuestions: Implement k-NN from scratch and explain its time/space complexity.
How would you speed up k-NN inference on millions of points without changing the prediction?

items.label: Code k-means from scratch
interviewQuestions: Implement k-means with k-means++ initialization.
Why does k-means++ initialization matter, and what does it cost vs random init?

items.label: Code logistic regression with SGD
interviewQuestions: Implement logistic regression with mini-batch SGD and L2 regularization.
What numerical-stability pitfalls do you watch out for when implementing the sigmoid + log-loss?

items.label: Code linear regression training from scratch
interviewQuestions: Implement linear regression training with vectorized gradients and a learning-rate parameter.
How do you verify your from-scratch implementation against sklearn or a closed-form OLS solution?

items.label: Creational: factory, builder, singleton
interviewQuestions: When is a singleton an anti-pattern in tests?
Compare factory vs abstract factory vs builder.

items.label: Structural: adapter, decorator, facade
interviewQuestions: When would you reach for an adapter vs a facade?
Where does the decorator pattern show up cleanly in an ML inference pipeline?

items.label: Behavioral: strategy, observer, command
interviewQuestions: Show how the strategy pattern lets you swap ML models without touching call sites.
When does observer cause subtle bugs in a high-throughput system?

tracks.label: ML Coding Lab · Production-quality notebook drills

items.label: Build a pandas/NumPy data pipeline with validation, leakage checks, and tests
interviewQuestions: Given messy CSV events, how do you build train rows without leaking future information?
What unit tests would you write for joins, deduplication, missing values, and label construction?
How do you make the notebook reproducible enough to hand to a production engineer?

items.label: Write a minimal PyTorch training loop with validation, checkpointing, and early stopping
interviewQuestions: Implement a training loop and explain where you call zero_grad, backward, step, and eval.
How do you avoid accidentally training with dropout or batch norm enabled during validation?
What checkpoint state do you save so training can resume exactly after interruption?

items.label: Modify a training loop to support gradient accumulation and mixed precision
interviewQuestions: How does gradient accumulation simulate a larger effective batch size?
Where do you divide the loss, step the optimizer, and clear gradients?
What can go wrong when gradient accumulation interacts with batch norm or learning-rate schedules?

items.label: Debug a broken ML notebook: no validation lift, unstable loss, and suspiciously high AUC
interviewQuestions: What is your debugging order when a model shows 0.99 validation AUC on a messy dataset?
How do you distinguish data leakage from a metric implementation bug?
Which plots or checks do you create before changing model architecture?

items.label: Review ML/Python code for readability, correctness, tests, and reproducibility
interviewQuestions: Walk through your review checklist for a messy ML PR: readability, type hints, hidden state, leakage, tests, and reproducibility.
Which Python mistakes do you look for first in interview code: mutable defaults, bare except, shadowing builtins, global mutation, or modifying lists while iterating?
How would you refactor a notebook cell that does parsing, feature engineering, training, logging, and plotting in one function?
What tests would you require before approving a vectorized NumPy or pandas rewrite of a loop?

references.label: Reddit — practical ML coding questions candidates report
